name: Excessive Login Failures via Network Connection
notes:
This rule triggers when multiple failed authentication events on the same machine from a single source IP address are detected.
This behaviour indicates a potential brute-force attempt to access a machine.

Note: General network authentication is processed as Logon Type 3 . This can cause the rule to be noisy so tune as needed.
bb_references: BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 5 times with the same Source Address
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Login Failure
when the event matches Logon Type (custom) is any of 3
when BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 5 times with the same Source Address, Destination Address in 5 minutes
thresholds: when BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 5 times with the same Source Address, Destination Address in 5 minutes
TD_list: APPLY Excessive Login Failures via Network Connection on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following Authentication.User Login Failure
AND when the event matches Logon Type (custom) is any of 3
AND when BB:CategoryDefinition: Authentication Failures match at least 5 times with the same Source Address, Destination Address in 5 minutes
RED_list: Event name: Excessive Login Failures via Network Connection to the same Machine
Event description: Multiple Windows Login Failures have been seen to the same machine.
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Machine Identifier (custom)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
